skill.name: project-scaffolder
skill.description: Scaffolds structured self-learning resource projects with templates for planning, tasks, personas, project context, and Claude instructions.
skill.summary: Builds coordinated documentation templates for new self-learning resource projects.
skill.author: BitYoungjae

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found Markdown templates, placeholders, and fenced examples rather than executable shell, Ruby, cryptography, or SAM database access. No prompt injection, credential access, network exfiltration, or malicious intent was found. The residual risk is low because the skill declares Bash permission and includes command placeholders for generated documentation.

low_findings.title: Broad Bash Permission for a Documentation Scaffolder
low_findings.description: The skill metadata allows Bash even though the reviewed workflow only needs to read templates and write documentation files. This is not evidence of malware, but the permission is broader than necessary for the documented scaffolding task.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools declaration directly includes Bash. Review found no actual command execution instructions, so the concern is limited to excess permission scope.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Treated as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick locations are Markdown command names, file paths, fenced examples, or placeholder values. No evidence found of dynamic command execution, user input interpolation into a shell, or executable Ruby code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are visible Markdown templates and placeholders. They do not define runnable scripts or invoke shell commands during skill use.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Placeholder Text Treated as Sensitive or Weak Crypto Access
low_findings.description: The reported Windows SAM and weak cryptography hits come from placeholder names and ordinary words such as description, system, and session. No evidence found of SAM database access, password dumping, MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other cryptographic implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are template labels or placeholder fields, not security-sensitive code. A targeted keyword review found no concrete crypto or SAM access behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation Checks Treated as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The reported reconnaissance lines describe frontmatter review, prohibited phrase placeholders, and completion criteria for generated documents. No evidence found of host enumeration, environment probing, or system information collection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is documentation quality control, not runtime discovery. The skill stores project context supplied by the user but does not query the host system.

content.value_statement: Starting structured learning documentation from a blank folder is slow. This skill creates coordinated planning, task, persona, context, and Claude instruction templates.
actual_capabilities: Creates a template set for plan.md, task.md, persona.md, project-context.md, and CLAUDE.md.
Organizes learning resources into parts, chapters, sections, and page allocations.
Defines task sessions with checklist items, progress summaries, and session boundaries.
Captures writer persona, reader profile, domain guidance, and terminology policy.
Records target environment, reference URLs, dependencies, and content freshness notes.
Provides CLAUDE.md workflow guidance for writing, status checks, and validation.
limitations: Requires project details from the user before the templates are useful.
Does not verify that generated setup commands are safe or correct.
Does not research source URLs unless another tool or agent performs that work.
Template placeholders need review before publishing final learning materials.

use_cases.title: Plan a Technical Guide
use_cases.description: Create a structured plan, task list, and context file for a new developer tutorial.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Course Outline
use_cases.description: Break a long learning resource into parts, chapters, sections, and production sessions.

use_cases.title: Standardize Documentation Projects
use_cases.description: Give a team consistent templates for personas, references, progress tracking, and Claude instructions.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Scaffold
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new learning resource.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use project-scaffolder to create templates for a 50-page beginner guide about Python automation in English.

prompt_templates.title: Add Audience and Scope
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate setup with clearer reader needs.
prompt_templates.prompt: Scaffold a 100-page Kubernetes guide for backend engineers. Include beginner-to-intermediate assumptions and session-based tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Detailed Course Structure
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced outline with environment and references.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create the full scaffold for a 200-page Rust systems programming course. Include Linux target environment, official references, and persona guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Customize a Production Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced use for team documentation standards.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a scaffold for a team learning project. Align plan.md, task.md, persona.md, project-context.md, and CLAUDE.md with our review workflow.

output_examples.input: A beginner Git guide with 50 pages and English language output.
output_examples.output: A coordinated scaffold with a chapter plan, session checklist, reader persona, project context, and Claude workflow notes.

output_examples.input: A 200-page cloud architecture course for intermediate engineers.
output_examples.output: A multi-part course structure with page allocation, task sessions, environment notes, reference tracking, and quality criteria.

output_examples.input: A documentation team wants a repeatable template for new tutorials.
output_examples.output: Reusable project files that define planning structure, writing standards, progress tracking, and project-specific Claude guidance.

best_practices: Provide topic, page count, language, audience, and target environment before scaffolding.
Review every placeholder before using the scaffold as a source of truth.
Keep task.md synchronized with plan.md after changing the project structure.
anti_patterns: Do not run generated setup commands without separate review.
Do not treat placeholder references as verified sources.
Do not use the scaffold as final content without editing and validation.

faq.question: What files does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates templates for plan.md, task.md, persona.md, project-context.md, and CLAUDE.md.

faq.question: Is this only for Claude Code?
faq.answer: It includes Claude Code workflow guidance, but the planning templates can also help Claude and Codex users.

faq.question: Can it write the final learning content?
faq.answer: It scaffolds the project structure. Writing final sections requires separate drafting and review.

faq.question: Does it install dependencies?
faq.answer: No. It documents dependency placeholders and setup commands, but those commands need human review.

faq.question: Can I change the page allocation?
faq.answer: Yes. The templates are designed for editing parts, chapters, sections, and page counts.

faq.question: What project details should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the topic, total pages, language, target audience, target environment, and preferred references.
